reasoning: The base rate of a U.S. stock going up over a random 1-week period is roughly 52-53%. The yfinance statistical model estimates a fairly high chance of recovery (>0.55) due to mean reversion and cyclical seasonality. However, NCLH experienced a dramatic fall over the past two weeks, plunging from ~$25 to $18.87 following poor Q4 earnings on March 2nd, lowered 2026 EPS guidance due to Caribbean overcapacity, and rising oil prices stemming from Middle East conflicts. Strong negative momentum provides strong headwinds. Yet, the stock has hit a technical level that some term a 'bearish floor' around $19 and is extremely oversold. Analyst targets remain above $23, and Elliott Management has taken a 10% activist stake, which could stabilize the price or induce a short-covering bounce. Given the short 1-week horizon, the generic base rate and technical rebound probabilities slightly outweigh the risk of continued straight-line selling, so I maintain an estimate around 0.52.
